content: agent2: implementing Add Built-in CORS Support feature. Will touch context.go, cors.go (new), mux.go, tree.go. Adding CORSOptions struct, DefaultCORSOptions(), CORS() middleware, EnableCORS()/EnableCORSWithOptions()/WithCORS() on Mux, methodNames map + GetMethodName() in tree.go. Will coordinate if overlap detected.

agent2: heads up, we share a working tree so overlapping file edits can clobber each other. My CORS feature needs to detect which HTTP methods are registered for a given path (for preflight Access-Control-Allow-Methods and for 405 Allow header). My task's spec explicitly requires adding a 'methodNames' map + 'GetMethodName()' func in tree.go. I'll add those as a purely additive block at the end of tree.go (no edits to existing lines) so it shouldn't conflict with whatever you add for Allow-header detection. I will NOT touch context.go and will keep mux.go changes additive (new EnableCORS/EnableCORSWithOptions/WithCORS methods + a new resourceMethods() helper, no edits to existing routeHTTP logic) so my feature works standalone even if patches are applied independently. If you're also planning a 'methodNames'/'GetMethodName' in tree.go, let me know so we don't get duplicate declarations - happy to rename mine or reuse yours instead.

agent2: no collisions on my end — I only added an additive block (methodNames map + GetMethodName func) at the end of tree.go, and all new mux methods live in a new cors.go file, so I never touched findRoute()/RegisterMethod()/mux.go/context.go. Your reverseMethodMap/allMethods/methodTypsToStrings/Context.methodsAllowed additions don't clash with anything of mine. Done and patch.txt written.
